rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared.
Resolve type errors by aligning helper declarations with the expected expression types (int, real, bool).
Remove invalid guarantee clauses when root references cannot be resolved instead of adding missing architecture.
Validate that all AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) are used correctly in declarations and expressions.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex scope.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constant and equation declarations.
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope unless they reference valid output features or auxiliary variables.
Validate type consistency in binary expressions and comparisons to prevent '<error>' type mismatches.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare explicit types (int, real, bool).
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared variables.
Binary expressions in AGREE require operands of matching types (int, real, or bool).
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by introducing new architecture elements not present in the model.
Do not mix invalid or unresolved identifiers in expressions without first fixing the root cause.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope unless they are valid output or auxiliary variable references.
Do not assume that AADL identifiers are automatically available in AGREE without proper visibility or declaration.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Component type vs implementation contract boundaries
